text:
There once was a quiet town filled with dandelions. They’d cover the lawns, making everything look like a sunbeam's happy smile.

One sunflower decided to talk to a young girl named Lily. Through fluttery whispers, it told her tales of adventures. The dandelion fly went far and wide, collecting little seeds.

One day, those seeds bloomed into wise old owls with big, yellow eyes. They told Lily about the magic of friendship, how it could lift anything. She found a feather she had saved all her life.

Then they showed her how to fly. Together, they drifted on gentle gusts. That feather became her compass. Friends were everywhere—birds, wind, clouds—all whispering tales.

Years passed. Lily became a legend. People said she could hear owls sing.

One moonlit night, someone stole her feather. When it wasn’t found morning came, Lily sat sadly under the old oak. But the owls visited nightly,
